title: The Legend of Theophilus (Rawlinson tail-rhyme version)
summary: CME00082 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of Wilhelm Heuser's complete 1903 article, including its German introduction and comments and its edition of the anonymous Middle English tail-rhyme Theophilus poem. Heuser transcribed the unique copy in Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Rawl. poet. 225, ff. 11-15. A complete Google scan of the original Englische Studien 32 and a complete scan of a 1965 Johnson photographic reprint are cached and mapped to the article. Beverly Boyd's 1956 reassessment and the complete DIMEV v1.0 release are also cached. DIMEV identifies Boyd's 1955/1964 book as a later complete edition; its 1964 scan is available only through controlled digital lending. No public manuscript facsimile was found in the live Digital Bodleian catalog on 2026-07-12, so the manifest records separate whole-codex mediated-copying and professional-imaging routes and the exact ff. 11-15 work locus.
review_notes: The abstract medieval poem, Heuser's article, original journal volume, 1965 reprint, physical manuscript, later scholarship, DIMEV record, U-M encoding, and checked-in XML are distinct layers.
The poem is the cataloged work even though CME00082 retains Heuser's complete German article framing.
A cached complete printed volume is not a manuscript facsimile; no local manuscript images are present.
Local research caching does not by itself grant permission to redistribute scans or use them in commercial print-on-demand products.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00082
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00082; the file is 43,246 bytes.
The XML has one DIV1, three DIV2 elements, 107 poem LG elements, 643 poem L elements, six editorial-comparison L elements, 23 PB elements, and a 2016 structural revision note.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00082
entities.description: An electronic text of Heuser's complete 1903 article: German preface, Middle English poem, and German Bemerkungen. It represents the printed edition rather than manuscript images.
notes: The item page labels Heuser as author, but for the cataloged medieval poem he is editor, transcriber, and commentator rather than author.
The item contents explicitly expose preface, poem, and Bemerkungen divisions.

entities.label: Wilhelm Heuser, Eine neue mittelenglische Version der Theophilus-Sage (1903)
entities.description: The first publication of the Rawlinson tail-rhyme version. German introduction occupies printed pp. 1-5, the manuscript rubric and complete 107-stanza poem pp. 5-23, and the Bemerkungen close p. 23.

bibliographic.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, 'Eine neue mittelenglische Version der Theophilus-Sage,' Englische Studien 32 (1903): 1-23.
bibliographic.title: Eine neue mittelenglische Version der Theophilus-Sage

notes: Heuser identifies MS. Rawl. poet. 225 as a badly mutilated paper manuscript whose fragments were frequently remounted on paper.
He rejects Rutebeuf's play as the direct exemplar and leaves the version's transmission unresolved.

entities.label: Google/Internet Archive scan of the original Englische Studien 32 (1903)
entities.description: A complete 494-page color-appearance PDF of the original carrier volume, including covers, preliminaries, contents, Heuser's article, the remainder of the journal, and Google usage-notice pages.

entities.label: Johnson Reprint Corporation photographic reprint of Englische Studien 32 (1965)
entities.description: The first photographic reprinting of the 1903 journal volume, with new Johnson title and colophon leaves. It is a distinct 1965 publication even though Internet Archive labels the item as 1903.

bibliographic.citation: Englische Studien, vol. 32, first reprinting (New York and London: Johnson Reprint Corporation, 1965).

notes: The reprint states that it was printed in West Germany.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the 1965 Johnson reprint of Englische Studien 32
entities.description: A complete 470-page scan of the photographic reprint. The item metadata calls it a 1903 volume, but visual inspection of its added preliminaries proves the 1965 carrier.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Rawl. poet. 225
entities.description: The sole known witness of this tail-rhyme Theophilus version, at ff. 11-15, in an imperfect South English Legendary and saints' lives miscellany. Heuser reports a mutilated paper codex with fragments remounted on paper.

physical_description.extent: Target work at ff. 11-15; the official catalog does not state a total leaf count
notes: Official catalog: an imperfect South English Legendary with additional saints' lives, some unique to this manuscript.
Heuser: strongly mutilated, with fragments and scraps frequently pasted onto new paper; exact current conservation structure awaits image or curatorial verification.
DIMEV reports LALME/eLALME Linguistic Profile 73, grid 548 300, localized to Ely.
DIMEV locates the Latin incipit rubric on f. 11 and 'Explicit Tyofle' on f. 15.

entities.label: Beverly Boyd, The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin (1955)
entities.description: A later complete edition of the Rawlinson poem on pp. 68-87, as identified by DIMEV. The present audit did not establish whether Boyd transcribed the manuscript directly or revised Heuser.

bibliographic.citation: Beverly Boyd, ed., The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin (New York: Columbia University Press, 1955), 68-87.
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin

entities.label: Beverly Boyd, The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin (Huntington Library, 1964)
entities.description: The Huntington Library issue of Boyd's edition. DIMEV groups it with the 1955 Columbia publication; the accessible Internet Archive metadata identifies a 1964 San Marino edition.

bibliographic.citation: Beverly Boyd, ed., The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin (San Marino, CA: Huntington Library, 1964).
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin

entities.label: Internet Archive controlled-lending scan of Boyd's 1964 edition
entities.description: A complete scan of the 1964 Huntington Library book. Anonymous access exposes encrypted lending files and metadata, while the unencrypted PDF and OCR are private; no local copy was cached.
entities.reproduction_format: Controlled-digital-lending page images and encrypted PDF

entities.label: Beverly Boyd, The Rawlinson Version of Theophilus (1956)
entities.description: A four-page critical note with short extracts, not a complete edition. Boyd distinguishes three Middle English versions and argues that tail-rhyme romance conventions better explain this version's dialogue and narrative handling than a direct miracle-play source.

bibliographic.citation: Beverly Boyd, 'The Rawlinson Version of Theophilus,' Modern Language Notes 71.8 (1956): 556-559.
bibliographic.title: The Rawlinson Version of Theophilus

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Modern Language Notes 71.8 (1956)
entities.description: A complete 97-page microfilm-derived scan of the issue containing Boyd's article at printed pp. 556-559, PDF pages 10-13.

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse record 3109 (v1.0)
entities.description: The current versioned work record for IMEV/NIMEV 1883, with unique-witness locus, manuscript titles, form, rhyme pattern, subjects, linguistic localization, and bibliography.
notes: Record 3109's descNote points to records 10 and 5061 as other versions. Record 5061 is actually an unrelated advice poem in this release; record 5554 is the South English Legendary Theophilus and reciprocally points to 3109.

Photographically reprinted Heuser article at printed pp. 1-23

object.description: Heuser's complete article and poem edition

scope.notes: Boyd explicitly cites Heuser as the first editor and quotes lines from the poem. This does not prove direct consultation of the manuscript.
A descriptive scholarly relation only; the article does not state that Boyd transcribed or collated the manuscript.

DIMEV record 3109 and linked manuscript entry BodRawPoe225

access.access_method: Bodleian Medieval Manuscripts catalog and source TEI
Digital Bodleian live search API and collection search
notes: Accept: application/ld+json searches for the exact shelfmark, Summary Catalogue number 14716, manuscript title variants, and Theophilus returned totalItems=0.
This is a dated negative public-access finding, not a claim that no institutional photography or preservation surrogate exists.

access.access_method: Bodleian Special Collections mediated copying order form submitted by email or post
access.contact: mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk; Reader Services, Weston Library, Bodleian Libraries, Broad Street, Oxford OX1 3BG, UK
access.format: Reference-quality PDF scans with shelfmark and folio metadata
notes: Available to all, subject to size, condition, legal restrictions, and scanning capacity; some material cannot be scanned.
Checked price: £1 per scan for non-Oxford users, with a £10 minimum; average turnaround about 10 working days.

For a complete source request, specify covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, edges if offered, and every manuscript leaf; separately identify ff. 11-15 as the work locus. Heuser's description of mutilation makes conservation approval especially material.

local_copies.label: Bodleian Imaging Services terms of use

access.access_method: Bodleian Imaging Services order form submitted by email or post
access.contact: Imaging Services, Bodleian Libraries, Broad Street, Oxford OX1 3BG, UK; specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for manuscript enquiries
notes: Checked order-form prices: £27 for the first image, £7.50 for subsequent images 2-99, £6.15 for subsequent images 100+, and a £20 minimum. Large or difficult orders are quoted separately and prepaid.
Standard turnaround is 20 working days from payment, subject to retrieval, fragility, security, and handling; the requested manuscript's mutilated condition may prevent or limit imaging.
Request the complete physical object and separately map ff. 11-15; do not describe a target-folio order alone as a complete manuscript facsimile.

local_copies.label: Bodleian image order terms and conditions

access.format: Onsite consultation of the physical manuscript, subject to access and conservation approval
Encrypted lending PDF and browser page images
notes: Anonymous metadata inspection reports access-restricted-item=true; the unencrypted PDF and OCR files are private. No authenticated access was bypassed and no local copy was made.
DIMEV places the complete Rawlinson poem edition at pp. 68-87.

rights.component: Repository XML serialization and encoding contributions
rights.basis: The pinned Kyvero-Press/CME repository root LICENSE applies the Unlicense public-domain dedication to the repository software. Source texts, third-party editions, scans, and provider terms remain separate layers.

U-M says the materials are public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright - United States statement; this does not automatically determine rights outside the United States or in third-party scan files.

rights.component: Underlying anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English text
rights.jurisdiction: United States, United Kingdom, and ordinary life-plus-70 jurisdictions
rights.basis: The anonymous text is preserved in a 1450-1500 witness and predates modern copyright terms by centuries. This does not grant rights in modern photographs, editions, transcriptions, or provider interfaces.

rights.component: 1903 article text, edition, commentary, and typography
rights.basis: The article was published in 1903, and both U-M and the Google scan identify the underlying book as public domain. Heuser died in 1924, but no worldwide claim is needed here.

rights.component: Google scan file, watermark, and added usage notice
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and unspecified copyright jurisdiction
rights.basis: The file calls the book public domain but requests personal non-commercial use, no automated querying, and retention of the Google watermark. No separate open license for the scan file was found.
notes: The requested-use language is recorded separately from copyright in the 1903 article; local caching is not permission to redistribute the PDF.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The reprint is a modern 1965 publication with added preliminaries and no verified item-level reuse license. The inherited 1903 article remains a separate public-domain component in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and metadata
rights.basis: The public item supplies no scan-file license, and its 1903 metadata obscures the 1965 reprint carrier.

rights.component: Boyd's modern edition, introduction, notes, and typography
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The 1955/1964 scholarly edition is modern and Internet Archive restricts the scan to controlled digital lending; no open license or United States renewal-status determination was verified.
notes: The medieval poem itself remains public domain; do not copy Boyd's editorial matter without a separate basis.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The article is modern scholarship, and the public Internet Archive issue supplies no item-level open license. No renewal or other copyright-status determination was completed in this audit.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and metadata
rights.basis: Public download is available, but no distinct open license for the scan files was found.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 data files and documentation
rights.basis: The release README and Zenodo metadata apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Reference scans of out-of-copyright material supplied through Bodleian mediated copying
rights.basis: The current mediated-copying form says scans of out-of-copyright material are © Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; current terms allow specified non-commercial and academic uses and require permission for commercial uses.
notes: The license is noncommercial and does not authorize for-profit print-on-demand reuse.
Confirm the license and permitted repository use in the item-specific delivery correspondence.

rights.component: High-resolution images of out-of-copyright material supplied by Bodleian Imaging Services
rights.basis: The current order forms say scans of out-of-copyright material are © Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; current terms allow specified non-commercial and academic uses and require permission for commercial uses.
notes: The license is noncommercial and does not authorize for-profit print-on-demand reuse.
A separately linked older Image Order Terms PDF says all reproduction requires written permission; this unresolved conflict is recorded below.

rights.component: Images supplied under the linked Image Order Terms and Conditions
rights.basis: The separately linked Image Order Terms and Conditions state that Bodleian retains copyright in all reproductions and that customers may not reproduce supplied material for any reason without written permission. This conflicts with the current forms and Terms of Use.
notes: Obtain item-specific written clarification before redistributing ordered images in the Git repository or using them commercially.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviations and several scribal flourishes
editorial_practices.representation: Abbreviations expanded except þu; r flourish represented by superscript e; a line over n interpreted as -e; an es flourish represented by superscript s
editorial_practices.description: Heuser explicitly states his expansion system in the Bemerkungen. The printed superscripts belong to the edition's representation and are not facsimiles of manuscript letterforms.
notes: CME00082 preserves many printed superscripts with SUP markup, so these typographic distinctions should not be flattened without review.
Heuser also encloses supplied letters in square brackets where manuscript readings were rubbed or obscured and lists doubtful readings in the Bemerkungen.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Frequent manuscript points in the middle of verse lines
editorial_practices.representation: Manuscript points retained alongside modern editorial punctuation
editorial_practices.description: Heuser says he preserved the manuscript's frequent mid-line points while adding modern punctuation.

evidence.summary: Direct evidence for artifact path, identifiers, XML structure, retained article framing, page milestones, encoding metrics, and 2016 revision history.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c (The Unlicense).
evidence.summary: Pinned repository evidence for the Unlicense public-domain dedication applied to repository software; it does not settle rights in third-party source editions or images.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00082 item information.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the U-M electronic object, title, Heuser responsibility, 1903 Leipzig publication, public-domain/No Copyright - United States assertion, and preface-poem-Bemerkungen contents.

evidence.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, 'Eine neue mittelenglische Version der Theophilus-Sage,' Englische Studien 32 (1903): 1-23; complete original-volume Google scan hosted by Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 1-23 = PDF pages 23-43; poem pp. 5-23 = PDF pages 27-43
evidence.summary: Complete primary evidence for the original article, its bibliographic facts, manuscript identification and condition, full transcription, source discussion, editorial method, and closing dateline. Added Google pages assert public-domain status while requesting personal non-commercial use and watermark retention.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the cached original Englische Studien 32 scan.
evidence.summary: Confirms original 1903 O. R. Reisland issue title and contents, article start and end, poem boundaries, color-appearance scan, and exact PDF-to-print page mapping.

evidence.citation: Visual and textual inspection of Internet Archive item englische-studien_1903_32.
evidence.locator: Added preliminaries; Heuser article printed pp. 1-23 = PDF pages 9-31
evidence.summary: Confirms that the apparently 1903 item is Johnson Reprint Corporation's first reprinting of 1965, printed in West Germany, and that the scan completely captures the reprint carrier and Heuser article.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts, MS. Rawl. poet. 225, manuscript_8763, Summary Catalogue 14716.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for current shelfmark, catalog IDs, paper support, English 1450-1500 origin, imperfect South English Legendary context, Richard Rawlinson provenance, and 1755 Bodleian acquisition.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian live search API queries for MS. Rawl. poet. 225 and related identifiers.
evidence.summary: Dated negative evidence: application/ld+json queries for the exact shelfmark, variants, Summary Catalogue 14716, South English Legendary, and Theophilus returned zero objects. The medieval catalog TEI contains no facsimile pointer.
notes: This supports 'no public copy found', not 'not digitized' in all institutional systems.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying, and current mediated-copying order form.
evidence.summary: Official evidence that all users may request reference-quality manuscript PDF scans, subject to condition and capacity, at £1 per scan with a £10 minimum; supplies email/post routes, turnaround, intended use, and current CC BY-NC statement for out-of-copyright scans.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services, and current Imaging Services order form.
evidence.summary: Official high-resolution order route, contact and delivery facts, £27 first-image, £7.50 images 2-99, £6.15 images 100+, £20 minimum, large-order quote, TIFF specifications, and condition-dependent fulfillment.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Imaging Services Terms of Use and reproducing-images guidance.
evidence.summary: Current provider terms permitting enumerated personal, non-commercial, teaching, and academic uses, including academic books up to 3,000 copies and non-commercial research websites, while requiring permission for commercial uses.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Image Order Terms and Conditions.
evidence.summary: Separately linked provider terms stating that Bodleian owns all reproductions and that customers may not reproduce supplied material for any reason without written permission; also states preservation and fragility limits.
notes: This conflicts with the current order forms and Terms of Use and therefore remains a separate rights layer and open question.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests.
evidence.summary: Official instructions for registration and requesting medieval manuscripts to Weston Library Level 1, subject to access and item restrictions.

evidence.citation: Beverly Boyd, 'The Rawlinson Version of Theophilus,' Modern Language Notes 71.8 (1956): 556-559.
evidence.summary: Specialist evidence for the Rawlinson-version title, distinction from other Middle English versions, fifteenth-century copy, Heuser first-edition status, plot, short extracts, and tail-rhyme-romance interpretation.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for Beverly Boyd, The Middle English Miracles of the Virgin (Huntington Library, 1964), item middleenglishmir0000boyd.
evidence.summary: Provider metadata confirms the 1964 San Marino Huntington Library edition, xviii plus 148 pages, and access-restricted-item=true; file metadata exposes encrypted lending files while the plain PDF and OCR are private.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, v1.0, record 3109, manuscript BodRawPoe225, and bibliography entries Heuser1903, Boyd1955, Boyd1956; DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14585961.
evidence.locator: Records.xml record-3109; Manuscripts.xml BodRawPoe225; Bibliography.xml Heuser1903, Boyd1955, Boyd1956
evidence.summary: Versioned specialist evidence for work identity, IMEV/NIMEV 1883, unique ff. 11-15 witness, manuscript titles, 107 six-line aabccb stanzas, subjects, LP 73 Ely localization, and known editions. README and Zenodo metadata apply CC BY 4.0.
notes: The record-3109 pointer to record 5061 is internally inconsistent; record 5061 is an advice poem, while record 5554 is the South English Legendary Theophilus and points back to 3109.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek/GND authority record for Wilhelm Heuser, GND 1029957312.
evidence.summary: Authority evidence identifying the educator and author Wilhelm Heuser, born 18 September 1864 and died 24 August 1924.

open_questions.question: Does Bodleian hold or plan a complete digital surrogate of MS. Rawl. poet. 225 outside the currently searchable Digital Bodleian catalog?
next_steps: Ask specialcollections.enquiries@bodleian.ox.ac.uk whether an institutional, preservation, or legacy surrogate exists and whether a complete source can be supplied.
If images become public, cache the entire published manuscript object and map ff. 11-15 rather than downloading only target leaves.

open_questions.question: Which current Bodleian terms control reuse of newly ordered images when the order forms and Terms of Use permit CC BY-NC or enumerated academic reuse but the separately linked Image Order Terms require written permission for every reproduction?
next_steps: Request item-specific written clarification before publishing ordered facsimiles in the Git repository or using them in any commercial or print-on-demand workflow.
Retain the exact supplied invoice, license, and correspondence as access-specific evidence if an order is fulfilled.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy of Englische Studien 32 was used to create the U-M encoding?
next_steps: Ask U-M whether production metadata identifies an original 1903 issue or a later photographic reprint; keep the encoding linked to the 1903 article text rather than to either cached scan until then.

open_questions.question: Did Boyd's 1955/1964 complete edition transcribe MS. Rawl. poet. 225 directly, revise Heuser, or combine both?
next_steps: Inspect Boyd's introduction and textual note through lawful controlled-lending or a library copy before adding a transcribes, collated_against, or version_of edge to the poem edition.

open_questions.question: Does the manuscript rubric on f. 11 read 'liberatus' as Heuser prints or 'deliberatus' as DIMEV records?
next_steps: Inspect a legible image of f. 11 or request a curatorial reading; preserve both sourced forms in descriptive metadata until then.

open_questions.question: Should DIMEV record 3109's pointer to record 5061 be corrected to record 5554?
next_steps: Report the internal release-data discrepancy to DIMEV and avoid treating unrelated record 5061 as a Theophilus version.

open_questions.question: What earlier textual source, if any, directly underlies this unique tail-rhyme version?
next_steps: Review later comparative scholarship; do not create an upstream edge to Rutebeuf, the Northern Homily Cycle, or the South English Legendary without direct dependency evidence.
